name: Argamasilla Trail
name_original: De Tomelloso a Argamasilla de Alba — circular
description: Hike 11.1 miles (17.9 km) on this moderate circular route from Tomelloso to Argamasilla de Alba, exploring La Mancha's vineyards and history
region_intro: Hiking around Tomelloso offers opportunities to explore the local scenery, characterized by its extremely flat terrain. The region features accessible paths through the general countryside, providing a straightforward outdoor experience. While lacking dramatic elevation changes, the landscape allows for enjoyable walks through open agricultural areas. Best hiking trails around Tomelloso The most popular hiking route is Argamasilla Trail, a 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects Tomelloso with Argamasilla de Alba. Another top favourite among local hikers is Bullring loop from Tomelloso, an easy 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route offers a gentle walk on easily accessible paths around the town. Local hikers also love the Tomelloso Chimney loop from Tomelloso, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through the flat, open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking around Tomelloso is defined by its extremely flat terrain, accessible paths, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Tomelloso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Tomelloso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tomelloso?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Tomelloso, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The majority of these, around 30, are considered easy, with a good selection of moderate routes and a few more challenging options.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on hikes around Tomelloso?
A: The landscape around Tomelloso is characterized by its &lt;strong&gt;extremely flat terrain&lt;/strong&gt; and open countryside. You can expect accessible paths that wind through rural agricultural areas, providing a straightforward outdoor experience without significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy hiking options in Tomelloso?
A: Yes, Tomelloso offers many easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bullring loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.7 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tomelloso Chimney loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 6.8 km trail. These routes are suitable for most fitness levels and feature easily accessible paths.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Tomelloso?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bullring loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tomelloso Chimney loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207395894/argamasilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a moderate circular route connecting Tomelloso with Argamasilla de Alba.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking near Tomelloso?
A: While the immediate area is flat, you can explore several natural attractions nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;. These offer pleasant views and a change of scenery from the open countryside.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, you can combine your hikes with visits to historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2719681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical site. Within Tomelloso itself, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall of Tomelloso (Plaza de España)&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posada de los Portales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Tomelloso?
A: The hiking routes in Tomelloso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessible paths and the opportunity to experience the quiet, open countryside.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Tomelloso suitable for families with children?
A: Given the predominantly flat terrain and numerous easy routes, many trails around Tomelloso are suitable for families with children. The accessible paths make for pleasant, less strenuous walks, allowing families to enjoy the outdoors together.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tomelloso?
A: The best time for hiking in Tomelloso is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable walks. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be chilly.

Q: Are there any moderate hiking trails for those seeking a bit more challenge?
A: Yes, for those looking for a bit more challenge, there are moderate trails available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207395894/argamasilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.9 km route that takes approximately 4 hours and 30 minutes to complete, connecting Tomelloso with Argamasilla de Alba. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.7 km long.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Tomelloso?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, the general nature of the accessible paths through open countryside suggests that many routes would be suitable for dog walking. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them to respect other hikers and the local environment.
tips:
Haughtily rise the chimneys of the old alcohol factories. At the peak of this activity in Tomelloso, more than 100 came into operation.
With heights of up to 45 meters, they were used to evacuate the smoke from the large boilers that, through the combustion of wood or coal, provided the adequate temperature for the operation of the distillation coil.
They are declared assets to be conserved, as they are elements of industrial archeology belonging to a past stage of the industrialization of the city.

The Town Hall of Tomelloso, the seat of the town council, is located at number 1 of the Plaza de España in this Manchegan city. Its construction was completed in 1904, following the project of the Albacete architect Francisco Manuel Martínez Villena, carried out in 1901.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Casa_consistorial_de_Tomelloso

The warehouses are an institution founded in the late Middle Ages. Its main activity was the subsidization of bread in years of famine in order to guarantee its price, as well as bakery among walkers and the poor. To these functions is added, from s. XVII, the loan of wheat or money between farmers. Its maximum splendor occurs throughout the s. XVIII, producing its total disappearance at the end of the s. XX.

Beautiful square, located in front of the town hall of Argamasilla de Alba.
Very well equipped and well landscaped, it has robust trees.
Decorated with statues and monuments that honor the work "Don Quixote de La Mancha" and also the writer "Azorín".

Tomelloso has been a population that has always been distinguished by a great fondness for the Festival of the bulls. Before October 4, 1859, the day of the Fair in which the old bullring was inaugurated, bullfights were held in the streets of the town, raising platforms for it.
The land where the old square was built, which is the same where the current one is located, was ceded for this purpose by the brothers Tiburcio and Leoncio Peinado, predecessors of the current manufacturers of the famous cognac and founders of said industry.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the circular hike?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the towns of Tomelloso or Argamasilla de Alba. As it's a circular route, you can begin from either town. Look for public parking areas near the town centers to easily access the trail and local amenities.

faqs.question: Is the From Tomelloso to Argamasilla de Alba circular hike dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and always clean up after your pet. Remember to bring enough water for both yourself and your dog, as water sources may be limited along the route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this route are during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for walking. Summers can be very hot in La Mancha, and winters can be chilly, though the flat terrain makes it accessible year-round with appropriate clothing.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to hike this circular route. It traverses public paths and rural roads between the towns.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Tomelloso to Argamasilla de Alba circular hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is notably flat, characteristic of the La Mancha region. You'll primarily walk on rural roads and tracks, likely compacted earth, through agricultural landscapes dominated by vineyards. There are no significant climbs or descents, making it a comfortable walk.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history and culture. In Tomelloso, you can see the unique Tomelloso Chimney, the Town Hall of Tomelloso (Plaza de España), and the Plaza de España. You'll also encounter the 'Bombos,' unique dry-stone constructions in the vineyards. In Argamasilla de Alba, explore the Casa Cueva Medrano, where Cervantes is believed to have started Don Quijote, and the La Tercia Granary.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its extremely flat terrain and moderate difficulty, this trail is suitable for beginners and families with older children who are accustomed to longer walks. The path is generally easy to navigate, offering a pleasant experience without challenging climbs.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails. Significant portions of it follow the Cañada Real de Cuenca o Vereda Soriana (27%) and the Camino de Argamasilla (26%). Smaller sections also align with the Sendero Priorato de San Juan (6%) and the Camino Natural del Guadiana (5%).

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route or in the towns?
faqs.answer: While there might not be facilities directly on the rural sections of the trail, both Tomelloso and Argamasilla de Alba offer a variety of cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel before or after your hike. It's advisable to carry snacks and sufficient water for the duration of your walk.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate length and flat terrain, comfortable walking shoes are essential. Dress in layers suitable for the season, and always bring sun protection (hat, sunscreen) as much of the route is exposed. Carry plenty of water, snacks, and a fully charged phone. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: What makes the landscape around Tomelloso and Argamasilla de Alba unique?
faqs.answer: The landscape is characterized by its vast, flat agricultural plains, particularly its extensive vineyards, which are central to the region's viticultural heritage. A unique architectural feature you'll encounter are the 'Bombos,' dry-stone constructions used as shelters by agricultural workers, which are considered rural architectural jewels.

waypoints.name: Town Hall of Tomelloso (Plaza de España)
